title: [P1] Workspace pane only opens halfway
body:
## Finding
Dragging to open the workspace pane stops at ~50% width and refuses to go full width.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Click an item to open the workspace pane
2. Drag the resize handle toward full width

## Expected
Workspace pane can be dragged to any width including full-width.

## Actual
Drag stops at approximately 50% — full width is unreachable.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Workspace pane)

title: [P1] Workspace pane close gesture broken
body:
## Finding
Dragging the workspace pane handle to close it does nothing.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Open the workspace pane
2. Drag the resize handle toward the closed position

## Expected
Pane closes (collapses) on drag.

## Actual
No response — pane stays open.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Workspace pane)

title: [P2] View toggle group options invisible
body:
## Finding
The toolbar view toggle group (grid / list / etc.) renders its container but shows no options — appears empty.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Navigate to any page with the view toggle (e.g. Media page)
2. Inspect the toggle group in the toolbar

## Expected
Grid, list, and other view options are visible and selectable.

## Actual
Toggle group container renders but has no visible items.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Workspace pane)

title: [P2] Toolbar view toggle group gets clipped when space is tight
body:
## Finding
When horizontal space is limited, the toolbar shows Filter / Sort / Grouping but the view toggle group after them is cut off rather than adapting.

## Expected
View toggle should stack, collapse to a cycle-button, or otherwise remain accessible at any viewport width.

## Actual
View toggle is clipped/hidden when other toolbar items consume available space.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Workspace pane)

title: [P3] View toggle: consider cycling-button UX when space is limited
body:
## Suggestion
Instead of clipping the view toggle group on smaller screens, replace it with a single button showing the current active view that cycles through views on click.

This avoids overflow issues while keeping view switching accessible.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Workspace pane)

title: [P3] Upload panel inside workspace reuses map-sidebar components inappropriately
body:
## Finding
The upload panel inside the workspace pane reuses components designed for the narrower map sidebar. The workspace context offers significantly more horizontal space and needs its own layout thinking.

## Expected
Upload panel in workspace pane has a layout optimized for its wider context.

## Actual
Layout is constrained as if in the map sidebar.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Workspace pane)

title: [P2] Account name missing in sidebar bottom section
body:
## Finding
The account / avatar area at the bottom of the navigation sidebar shows no user name.

## Expected
User name (or display name) is visible alongside the avatar.

## Actual
No user name rendered — area appears incomplete.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Navigation sidebar)

title: [P2] Active nav item hover state inconsistent — icon color changes on hover
body:
## Finding
Active nav item state: icon + text both orange with light-orange background.
Hovering over the active item: only the text stays orange; the icon treatment changes (loses orange color).

Icon and text should have the same color treatment in all states including active+hover.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Navigation sidebar)

title: [P2] i18n: "Media" nav label wrong German translation — should be "Medien" not "Medium"
body:
## Finding
The German translation for the "Media" navigation item is "Medium" (singular/material). It should be "Medien" (plural/collection).

## Correct translations
- English: `Media` (unchanged)
- German: `Medien` (currently wrong: `Medium`)
- Italian: `Media` (unchanged)

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Navigation sidebar)

title: [P3] Media nav item uses camera icon — should use content/elements icon
body:
## Finding
The "Medien" (Media) nav item uses a camera icon. This represents a photo-capture action rather than a media collection/library.

## Expected
Use an icon that represents a content collection or media library (e.g. images/grid/elements icon).

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Navigation sidebar)

title: [P2] Media toolbar wraps unexpectedly — "Kopieren" drops to row 2
body:
## Finding
On the Media page toolbar, Filter + Sort + Projects render on row 1, but "Kopieren" (Copy) wraps to a second row despite sufficient horizontal space.

## Expected
All toolbar items fit on a single row.

## Actual
Layout breaks — "Kopieren" wraps to row 2.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Media page)

title: [P1] Media items not rendering in main view
body:
## Finding
Media items in the main media grid/list view do not display correctly.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Navigate to the Media page
2. Observe the main content area

## Expected
Media items (photos, files) render in the grid or list.

## Actual
Items do not display correctly — content area appears broken.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Media page)

title: [P1] "Zeilen" (rows/list) view not implemented — shows single large image instead of list
body:
## Finding
Selecting the "Zeilen" (list/rows) view on the Media page shows a single large image instead of a list layout.

## Expected
List view renders items as rows with metadata.

## Actual
A single oversized image is displayed — list layout is unimplemented.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Media page)

title: [P1] Clicking media item doesn't open workspace pane
body:
## Finding
Clicking a media item (image or file) on the Media page does nothing — the workspace detail pane does not open.

## Expected
Clicking a media item opens the workspace pane with item details.

## Actual
No response — workspace pane is not triggered.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Media page)

title: [P2] Upload FAB should be persistent in top-right of content area
body:
## Finding
The upload button should be a persistent FAB (floating action button) anchored to the top-right of the content area, always visible regardless of navigation state or whether the workspace pane is open.

Currently it is placed inside the workspace pane or otherwise not persistent.

## Expected
Upload FAB: always visible, top-right of content area, present across navigation like the side menu.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Media page)

title: [P3] Projects page missing structural breakdown / hierarchy view
body:
## Finding
The Projects page lacks a structural breakdown or hierarchy view. There is no way to see project nesting, grouping, or organizational hierarchy.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Projects page)

title: [P3] Toggle group "Alle / Archiviert" on Projects page poorly styled
body:
## Finding
The "Alle / Archiviert" (All / Archived) toggle group in the top-left of the Projects page looks aesthetically poor — inconsistent with the design system.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Projects page)

title: [P3] "Neues Projekt" button incorrectly formatted / styled
body:
## Finding
The "Neues Projekt" (New Project) button on the Projects page is incorrectly formatted or styled — does not match design system conventions.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Projects page)

title: [P1] Project cards need complete redesign — DO NOT PATCH
body:
## Finding
Project cards have multiple visual and interaction problems. The user explicitly does **not** want individual patches applied.

## Decision
**Delete and rebuild from scratch.** Do not fix individual styling issues — the component needs a full redesign.

## ⚠️ Do not patch individual issues on project cards
Any PR that patches a subset of project card issues will be rejected. Only a complete redesign commit is acceptable.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Projects page)

title: [P2] Filter dropdown has excess right padding (scrollbar-placeholder duplication)
body:
## Finding
The Projects page filter dropdown has unexplained excess right padding — same scrollbar-placeholder duplication issue as the search results dropdown (see #49).

## Expected
Filter dropdown right edge aligns without extra padding.

## Actual
Extra right padding creates visual imbalance.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Projects page)

title: [P2] Project color picker button does not work
body:
## Finding
Clicking the color swatch / color picker button on a project card has no effect.

## Expected
Color picker opens and allows changing the project color.

## Actual
No response — button does nothing.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Projects page)

title: [P2] Clicking project card should open workspace panel with project detail view
body:
## Finding
Clicking a project card does not open the workspace panel. A project detail view needs to be wired to the project card click.

## Expected
Clicking a project card opens the workspace panel showing project details.

## Actual
No workspace panel opens — click has no effect.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Projects page)

title: [P3] Settings section highlight has square corners
body:
## Finding
The color indicator / active highlight on selected settings items (Language, Density, etc.) has square corners. It should use rounded corners consistent with the design system.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Settings overlay)

title: [P3] Settings section highlight bleeds over white section background
body:
## Finding
The orange active highlight on settings items overlays the white section background, creating an ugly visible boundary.

## Expected
Highlight respects the section background boundary, or the approach is changed to avoid the bleed entirely.

## Actual
Orange highlight bleeds over white section background — visually broken.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Settings overlay)

title: [P3] Settings language options missing flag icons
body:
## Finding
The language selection options (English, Deutsch, Italiano) in the settings overlay have no flag or country icons.

## Expected
Each language option shows a small flag icon next to the language name.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Settings overlay)

title: [P2] Settings overlay opens with current route context instead of standalone /settings
body:
## Finding
Opening Account / Settings while on the map page navigates to a settings route that is nested under the current page (e.g. `/map/settings`). Settings should be an independent route.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Navigate to `/map`
2. Open settings overlay via Account menu
3. Observe URL

## Expected
Settings opens at `/settings` regardless of the current page route.

## Actual
Settings opens nested under the current route (e.g. `/map`), creating context-dependent behavior.

## Source
Phase 10 Visual QA — Part 2 (Settings route)
